Key Takeaways:
• It’s time to talk about Joint Venture (JV) launches, a popular topic among PLF
Owners. Now one thing to note is that these can get pretty complex, but they can
be very beneficial to your business too.
• At its most basic, a JV partner of yours will mail their list to look at your PLC, buy
your product, and you’ll give them a portion of the sales (commission). It’s a way
to get yourself in front of a lot of people
• These launches can be very lucrative. I’ve personally made tens of millions in sales
through these types of launches. Yes, it sounds awesome, but there are a lot of
potential issues
• As you know, PLF is all about a long-term success game plan. This is not about
getting rich quick. You must first build out your Internal Launch and make sure it
works - you need your metrics
• So for example, you have 3 pieces of PLC, and then a Sales Video. If you have a JV
partner, they would mail into a squeeze page and the people that opt in would
receive your first piece of PLC
• If they have a 10,000-person list and send 1,000 visitors to your squeeze page, and
that converts at 50%, you would have 500 people going through your launch
• Now imagine if you had 5 of these partners that each had 10,000 person lists! As
you can see, this is the fastest way to build your list
• If they buy from you then you give your partners commission
• It’s important to do that Internal Launch first - don’t test a launch on your
partners list. If you make a mistake you may burn your relationship with your
partner
• This is not a short-term game - I have partners that have been promoting me since
2008 when I first released Product Launch Formula. You have to get these
partners on board and that’s what we’re going to talk about now
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 7 --

3
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
• So, how do you find partners? Google is a great place to start. You probably
already know who your competitors (future partners) are
• Who are the people that are influencers or big dogs in your market? Track who
these people are and join their lists. Join their affiliate program – watch what they
send out to their affiliates and main lists
• Track who promotes who and understand what the JV landscape is like in your
market. I’m a big fan of live events, I like this strategy and it’s a great way to meet
people
• You can also go to Clickbank.com and search on their marketplace – you can see
what products are selling. Great launches can be done with just 5 very committed
partners
• If you get the right partners others will follow. It’s important to note that getting
them on board is difficult - entrepreneurs are always busy. You have to break
through that and build a relationship with them
• It’s the same in all businesses, you have to add value to them if you want to build
the relationship. You never want to ask someone to mail for you before you build a
relationship for them
• This strategy rarely works and it will annoy the person. Even if they do work with
you, they won’t fully invest their efforts into the launch the way someone you
know well would

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
Okay. Now we're going to talk about Joint Venture Launches. Joint Venture
Launches, boy, they can get pretty complex, but at the most basic, what it is is a joint
venture partner of yours or an affiliate, they will mail their list to go and look at your
Prelaunch Content, then the people from their list will go through your pre-launch, buy
your product, and then you'll give that joint venture partner some portion of the sales,
the proceeds, the commission.
The great thing about this is it's just a way to all of a sudden put yourself in front
of a lot of people. Just think about it. You might have a list of 1000 people that you've
built up over months or a year or whatever, but if you can find a partner that's got 10,000
people on their list, it's like, all of a sudden, you have extended your influence in a big
way. Joint Venture Launches can be really lucrative. I've personally made millions, in
fact, I've had tens of millions of dollars in sales in affiliate and JV Launches. Really
powerful stuff.
Now, it sounds awesome but there's a lot of "gotchas," and it's not like a quick fix
in general. What I'm going to give you is here in Product Launch Formula, as you've seen
throughout all of Product Launch Formula, it's all about the long term, having a long-
term win. It's not a get rich quick, and you can't look at joint venture partners as a get
rich quick type of thing. This is very much a holistic approach to your business. It's a
longer-term approach, but it absolutely works.
The first thing you've got to do is, you've got to build out your Internal Launch
first, and you need to get your metrics and make sure it works. Let's draw this out. We're
going to say that this is your launch. Okay. You've got three pieces of Prelaunch Content.
Goes like that. Here's Number One, Number Two, Number Three, and your Sales Video.
Typical classic Internal Launch.
What you want to do, if you remember back to that Circle of Awesome, which I
covered earlier in PLF, is this idea where you do your Internal Launch first. You figure out
what your metrics are, and you make sure the launch works, and then you move forward
and you do a Joint Venture Launch, because what a Joint Venture Launch is, is if this is
your Internal Launch, then say you have a joint venture partner out here with a list. What
they would do is they would mail into a Squeeze Page, and the people that opted in would
get your first piece of Prelaunch Content.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 7 --

5
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
If this person has a 10,000-person list and maybe they sent 1000 visitors to your
Squeeze Page, and you've got a really great Squeeze Page, just to make the math easier, at
a 50% opt-in rate, all of a sudden you would have 500 people going through your launch.
That is an awesome thing.
Now, think if you had five of those joint venture partners, and they each had
those 10,000 person lists, and they're all mailing into your Squeeze Page. You can see
how this could get very exciting very quickly. This is the absolute best way to build a list
because these people that come through your Squeeze Page, they're now on your list.
What you're going to do is you're going to track via software, and we'll get into
that a little bit later, but once they join your list and they run through these pieces of pre-
launch content, if they buy from you, then you are going to give your partners
commission based on the sales that you make to the people that sent to you.
This is the best list building. I can't remember if I said this, but I've done this over
and over, where I've added tens of thousands of people to my list within a couple of
weeks. It's very powerful. That's how I've built my list. It's the absolute best way to do it.
Now, like I said, there is some complexities here, and it doesn't work for every
business, but it works for a lot of them. The one thing you've got to do is you've got to do
the Internal Launch first so you get the metrics right. You don't want to ever test a launch
on your partner's list because if you put together a launch, and the launch is not a great
offer. It's not a great pre-launch or maybe your systems aren't in place, and you're
shopping cart isn't hooked up right, or some technical problem. If you make a mistake
with a Joint Venture Launch, these partners probably … You're going to burn your
relationship with them, and they're not going to come back and promote for you in the
long run.
Everything I teach is about building a long-term business that works going
forward and continues to build. The way you do that in a Joint Venture Launch is making
sure you get great results, so that when you Open Cart, you make a bunch of sales, and
you can pay your partners a bunch of money.
This is not a short-term game. I have partners that have been promoting ever
since I first released Parkland's Formula back in 2008. They were promoting me back
then, and they're still promoting me now. The way you do that is you approach it through
a very long-term focus, and part of that is doing the Internal Launch first.
Of course, to do any of this, you have to get these partners on board, and so that's
what we're going to talk about the rest of this video and the next video, and I think the
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 7 --

6
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
next video after that, because this is a really key thing is getting the partners so they're
willing to mail for you. If someone's got a 10,000-person list, they’ve got a lot of people
coming to them asking if they'll promote for them. That's the way it goes.
How do you find partners? This might sound really simplistic, but Google is one
of the greatest ways. Frankly, if you're in a market, you've been in that market for a while,
you probably have a good idea who your competitors are, and I use that term
"competitors." I really think of them not as competitors but as future partners. Who in
your market are the big dogs? Who are the people that are publishing? Who are the
people that are building lists?
Create a list of those. I use a spreadsheet to do this. Then, go to Google and do a
search on your main key word phrases and go and look at the top 20 or the top 50
listings, and visit every one of those sites and see, are they building a list? If they're not
building a list, they're probably not going to be a great venture partner or a great affiliate,
but are they building a list?
Go ahead and join their list. You probably want to put together, get a separate
Email account or separate Gmail account so you can join all their lists and watch what
they're doing. Then go in and join their affiliate programs. See do they have an affiliate
program. When you go to their Website, just do a search on their page and see if they've
got an affiliate program. Join their affiliate program. Watch what they send out to their
affiliates. Watch what they send out to their main list.
Then, like I said, create that spreadsheet and track them. Track who promotes
who, who's sending good emails, who's not doing a very good job of following up. You
really want to understand what the JV landscape is like within your market. Are these
top five all promoting each other? Or are they mostly, people aren't promoting each
other? You really need to focus in on that overall JV landscape.
Other ways to find them is, once you start to get that list of potential partners in
Google, another way is to go to live events. I'm a big fan of going to live events to meet
potential partners. You need to basically go there, and you're looking for who the players
are. Who are the people that have lists and are getting results? Go to ClickBank and
search on their marketplace. This doesn't work for all products and all markets, but a lot
of them, you can take a look at the marketplace and ClickBank and see what products
they're selling.
They have this measure called "Gravity," and that basically tells you what
products are selling well. Go in there and see who's got really good products that are
selling really well.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
These are some of the ways that you can start to figure out, who are the players?
Who can really make an impact in your market? One of the things is, like I said, I want
you to go through the top 50 sites and find out who's building a list and start building
relationships, but the reality is is you only need one partner.
I drew this out first with this one partner. I added in four more, but you could do
a nice JV Launch with just one partner, and even great launches can be done with five
really committed partners, and I'm talking million-dollar launches. If you get the right
five partners, they'll end up bringing in some other people on their coattails but you need
those five that are really committed. To start out, you really only need one that is really
committed.
Now, getting these people on board … The thing is, everyone is too busy these
days. Everyone, but entrepreneurs are even more busy than anyone else, and successful
entrepreneurs are completely, way too busy. You've got to break through that and build a
relationship with them if you're going to get them to promote for you.
Building a relationship in this business is no different from any other business
out there. You have to add value to them.
If you don't have enough partners, then you haven't added enough value to
enough other people's lives yet. You've got time, and the way you do this is one
relationship at a time. The next video's going to be all about building those relationships.
One thing you really want to avoid is what I call the "T-bone JV Marketing." Sometimes,
I'll go to an event and I'll feel like I've got a couple of T-bone steaks tied around my legs,
and there'll be people out there. They're like pit bulls that want me to promote for them,
and they see me as a piece of meat, and they're coming at me, and they want to eat those
T-bones that are tied to me. They're after me like this pit bull after this T-bone.
Not the way to do it. You don't want to be rushing up to someone and asking
them, "Mail for me." It doesn't work. You have to build relationships. You have to build
value in other people's lives, and that' what we're going to get to in the next video.
